systemArea: Foundation source access / God Mode extractor sessions
acceptanceState: Verified V1 broker contract and local proof accepted as blocked-preflight; live Harlan delivery, MyICOR connector approval, and broad paid/private extraction are approval-bound continuation work and the full Source Session Broker capability is not done.
whatChanged: Added the Source Session Broker V1 contract and proof so approved extractors have one closed-loop access layer for isolated profiles, macOS Keychain credential refs, source identities, login recipes, auth-needed escalation, wait/resume, and fail-closed behavior.
whatItDoes: Defines the source-session gold standard: try the existing isolated browser profile first, prefer a native read-only connector/MCP when the source offers one and scope is approved, use broker-only Keychain credentials when a login recipe exists, emit auth_needed on missing creds/login recipe/MFA, wait for the human-agent DONE signal, silently reverify, then resume or fail closed. It also extends the credential vault CLI with generic source add/status/delete and wires paid-source local mapping to auth-needed artifacts.
whyItMatters: Steve cannot be the login bottleneck for every Skool, MyICOR, newsletter, free community, or future team-agent source. God Mode extraction needs a reusable session layer that can safely access approved accounts while refusing purchases, trading, banking, posting, messaging, profile changes, unsafe downloads, and raw-secret leakage.
whereItLives: lib/source-session-broker.js broker contract, source policies, MyICOR read-only MCP preference, request evaluator, and dogfood proof
scripts/process-source-session-broker-check.mjs focused proof and guarded live backlog upsert
lib/source-session-readiness-readback.js metadata-only readiness checks for parked source-session action groups
scripts/process-source-session-readiness-check.mjs focused live readiness proof
docs/process/source-session-broker-001-plan.md approved process plan
docs/process/approvals/SOURCE-SESSION-BROKER-001.json approval record
scripts/credentials-vault.mjs generic source credential add/status/delete commands
scripts/run-supervised-paid-source-map.mjs paid-source browser/profile mapper and auth-needed artifact writer
lib/credential-vault.js macOS Keychain secretRef and broker-only retrieval helpers
lib/harlan-auth-escalation-loop.js auth_needed wait/reverify/fail-closed event contract
lib/foundation-backlog-seed-chunks/chunk-005.js Source Session Broker seed card
live backlog_items row for SOURCE-SESSION-BROKER-001
package.json focused proof command
proofStatus: Focused proof validates the broker contract, live P0 backlog card, approval integrity, seed truth, closeout registry, generic source credential CLI, source-session readiness readback, paid-source mapper auth-needed behavior, broker-only Keychain retrieval, MyICOR native read-only connector/MCP preference with explicit scope approval, missing credential and MFA fail-closed auth_needed paths, standing free-source identity account creation policy, forbidden money/trading action blocking, and no raw secret printing. Live readiness currently shows the parked source-session queue waiting on missing Skool/source-newsletter/myICOR MCP credentials or sessions, which is honest blocked-preflight readback. A local unattended MyICOR run currently stops with auth-needed when no authorized session/credential clears login, which is the expected fail-closed posture. This is a blocked-preflight closeout, not accepted full implementation completion.
reviewNext: Approve or configure the exact MyICOR read-only MCP/connector scope if available, then prove connector readback before browser fallback. Continue SKOOL-LIVE-NAVIGATION-PROOF-002 and MYICOR-LIVE-NAVIGATION-PROOF-002 for bounded source-specific extraction, and HARLAN-AUTH-LIVE-DELIVERY-002 for real auth notification delivery.
knownLimits: V1 is the broker/access contract and local proof, not broad paid/private extraction and not done as the full source-session capability.
This does not send live Telegram, Gmail, Slack, or email notifications; Harlan live delivery remains HARLAN-AUTH-LIVE-DELIVERY-002.
This does not approve or use the MyICOR MCP/connector until Steve confirms the exact read-only scope and account boundary.

This does not create live external accounts, sign up for newsletters, join Skool communities, buy, trade, bank, post, comment, message, mutate profiles, mutate credentials, download unsafe files, or write externally from the proof.
This does not store paid/private source content in git or expose raw secrets in reports.

systemArea: Foundation source access / source-session profile probe
acceptanceState: Blocked-preflight / approval-bound partial closeout: not done, not accepted as full SOURCE-SESSION-BROKER-001 completion, and not marked done. Approval is pending for live Harlan delivery, real Skool/MyICOR login completion, newsletter submit, paid/private extraction, and broad source-session runner execution. V1 proves only the local profile readiness/auth-needed/wrong-branch probe.
whatChanged: Added a local source-session profile probe that opens an exact source URL in an isolated Playwright source profile, reads page/auth/browser state, calls the Source Session Broker, and writes a local report that says ready, auth_needed, wrong_signup_branch, or blocked without submitting credentials.
whatItDoes: Gives the source system a concrete preflight between parked source rows and real source-specific extraction. It can prove an existing isolated profile is ready, detect a login wall, detect MyICOR Start Free/profile-creation drift, detect MyICOR Google SSO MFA/human verification, and prepare the existing auth-resume packet while sending no live Harlan message.
whyItMatters: Steve needs the system to stop getting lost on login/signup screens and to know when it can run unattended versus when it must ask for MFA through the human-agent loop. This slice turns that state into machine-readable proof before the Skool/MyICOR runners are allowed to claim extraction.
whereItLives: lib/source-session-profile-probe.js local profile probe, broker decision bridge, MyICOR auth-surface evaluation, auth-resume packet output, and report evaluator
scripts/run-source-session-profile-probe.mjs `source:session-probe` CLI

proofStatus: Focused proof covers four dogfood states: a ready free-community fixture returns broker `session_ready` with no auth escalation, a login-wall fixture returns `auth_needed` and a dry-run Harlan resume packet, a MyICOR Start Free/profile fixture fails closed as wrong signup branch, and a MyICOR number-match fixture returns Google SSO MFA auth-needed. All paths avoid login submit, model calls, Browserbase, normal Chrome, raw secrets, external writes, downloads, purchases, posting, messaging, and credential/profile mutation.
reviewNext: Use this probe before the first real Skool free-community and MyICOR source-session runs. If probe status is ready, run the bounded source-specific worker. If it is auth_needed, use the dry-run Harlan packet and reverify before resuming. If it is wrong_signup_branch or blocked, clear only the isolated source profile and retry the exact approved login path.
knownLimits: This is a source-session readiness/auth-state probe, not full source extraction.

This does not complete Google login, solve MFA, send Telegram, join Skool, sign up for newsletters, create external accounts, buy, trade, bank, download files, post, comment, message, mutate profiles, mutate credentials, or promote Scoper cards.
This does not use Browserbase or a hosted browser; Browserbase remains a separate one-month bakeoff only if local proof exposes a real missing capability.
This does not store paid/private content or raw secrets in git.

whatChanged: Source-session readiness now exposes `source:session-probe` commands for Skool free-community and MyICOR parked rows before the full source runners are attempted.
whatItDoes: Makes the source-session prep queue show the correct first move: probe the isolated source profile and broker decision, then run `skool:free-god-mode` or MyICOR extraction only after the probe returns ready. Skool probes use the exact community/about URL and `skool_free_community`; MyICOR probes use a myICOR URL, `paid_course_training_platforms`, and Steve existing Google SSO account.
whyItMatters: The system should not jump from missing credentials directly into full extraction. Steve needs the queue and UI to show whether the source is ready, auth-needed, wrong-branch, or blocked so the extractor can self-diagnose instead of getting stuck or pretending God Mode is done.

whatChanged: Source-session readiness now separates the paid MyICOR Google SSO credential from the MyICOR MCP OAuth token, shows accidental free-account MyICOR SSO rows as optional/ignored, and routes missing MCP-token guidance to the agent-driven OAuth command first.
whatItDoes: Makes the MyICOR readiness state plain: the existing paid Google SSO credential is checked as `myicor-google-sso / steve.zahnd@bensoncrew.ca`; the read-only connector token is checked as `myicor-mcp-oauth / myicor-authorized-member`; `myicor-google-sso / ai@bensoncrew.ca` is not a paid MyICOR route and never blocks readiness.
whyItMatters: Steve needs the system to know that MyICOR is Sign in with Google on the paid account, not a new account and not a free-source identity. This prevents the queue from looking stuck on the wrong credential and keeps the next MyICOR action pointed at the source-session broker path.

subjectIncludes: Treat newsletter source identity as delegated mailbox

systemArea: Foundation source access / creator newsletter source identity readiness
acceptanceState: Verified newsletter source identity readiness is accepted as blocked-preflight, not done as live newsletter signup, newsletter issue extraction, or full Source Session Broker completion. Live external newsletter submit, confirmation-mail monitoring, unsubscribe/park automation, Harlan live delivery, and broad source-session runner execution remain approval-bound continuation work.
whatChanged: Source-session readiness now treats creator newsletter source identity as delegated mailbox metadata under `SRC-GMAIL-001` instead of a fake Keychain password for `creator-newsletters / ai@bensoncrew.ca`. The readiness primary next action now points at the actual remaining missing token when only myICOR MCP OAuth is missing.
whatItDoes: Shows that the AIOS newsletter identity is the delegated `ai@bensoncrew.ca` mailbox and keeps newsletter intake as dry-run/form detection until the live signup/inbox lane exists. The live readiness count drops the bogus newsletter credential blocker while keeping MyICOR MCP OAuth and paid/auth packet boundaries visible.
whyItMatters: Steve approved using `ai@bensoncrew.ca` for free newsletters. Asking for a separate newsletter password creates false operator work and makes the queue look more blocked than it is. This keeps the system honest: mailbox identity is available, live external signup still needs the newsletter signup lane.

subjectIncludes: Route approval Skool links out of free queue

systemArea: Foundation source browser / Skool approval boundary routing
acceptanceState: Blocked-preflight repair: not done, not accepted as full SOURCE-BROWSER-AGENTIC-RUNTIME-001 completion, and not a full God Mode source extraction claim. This slice only fixes the routing boundary so approval-required Skool/community links do not masquerade as free-community runner work.
whatChanged: The YouTube handoff router now preserves approval/link-blocker metadata from watched-video evidence and lets that metadata beat generic community URL matching. A Skool/community URL that the extractor already marked approval-required now routes to paid/auth review instead of `free-communities`; explicit non-approval free-community evidence can still route to the free-community lane.
whatItDoes: Prevents paid/private or approval-required Skool pages, such as Maker School links discovered from Nick Saraev videos, from getting `skool:free-god-mode` run-after-session commands. Source-session readiness and Dev Hub proofs now allow the honest live state where there may be zero confirmed-free Skool prep rows, while still requiring newsletter and paid/auth readiness checks and no raw-secret/external-action side effects.
whyItMatters: Steve needs the system to move fast on free sources without lying about paid/private gates. The previous order let "skool" text override the approval-required evidence and made the dashboard look like a paid/private community was a free-community blocker. This repair keeps the source queue honest before any real Skool/MyICOR hands work runs.
whereItLives: lib/dev-team-hub.js handoff link metadata preservation and approval-before-community routing

proofStatus: Focused Dev Hub proof passes: a synthetic watched-video report routes `https://skool.com/makerschool/about` with approval-required source-packet evidence into `paid-auth-gates`, routes explicit `https://www.skool.com/chase-ai-community/about` free-community evidence into `free-communities`, and proves Maker School is not in the free-community dogfood row. Source-session readiness proof passes with metadata-only checks, 0 live external/auth/form actions, and the current live queue honestly waiting on newsletter and paid/auth readiness.
reviewNext: Continue SOURCE-SESSION-BROKER-001 and FREE-SKOOL-COMMUNITY-GOD-MODE-RUNNER-001 by proving exact free-community rows through source-session profile probes, then run the bounded 20-day Skool SOP only when a page is confirmed free and session-ready.

This does not log in, join communities, submit forms, sign up for newsletters, download files, buy, post, comment, message, mutate profiles, mutate credentials, write backlog, send Harlan messages, or promote Scoper cards.
